vocabulary.word: talk show / quiz show / game show

phrasal_verbs.meaning: try to find information in a book or list, etc
pretend that something is true; see, hear or understand sb or sth with difficulty
invent an explanation, excuse, etc; create a story, poem, etc
add information in the spaces on a document
turn and look at the pages of a magazine, etc quickly
recognise that sth is not true and not be tricked by it
be easy to see because of being different
give things to people in a group
turn a page so that the other side is towards you

question: ask/answer a question
question sth/sb
in question
question mark
comment: make a comment (to sb) about sth

D.title: Original key word transformation practice
D.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it keeps the same meaning as the first.

questions.sentence:
Mia began the class project before the lesson on The media. **OUT**
The class project ...... before the lesson on The media.

Leo began the study group before the lesson on The media. **UP**
The study group ...... before the lesson on The media.

Sara began the exam workshop before the lesson on The media. **ON**
The exam workshop ...... before the lesson on The media.

Tom began the online lesson before the lesson on The media. **INTO**
The online lesson ...... before the lesson on The media.

Nina began the school club before the lesson on The media. **THROUGH**
The school club ...... before the lesson on The media.

Oscar began the revision session before the lesson on The media. **UP**
The revision session ...... before the lesson on The media.

Iris began the practice notebook before the lesson on The media. **THROUGH**
The practice notebook ...... before the lesson on The media.
